working_on.task: Release the completed Forge v3 strict TDD and subagent execution tranche as Forge 2.11.0, sync continuity surfaces, then push to main

recent_changes: Added strict TDD enforcement with failing-test-first, delete-before-RED, named baseline, and no-harness fallback contracts.
Added packet-first subagent execution with implementer, spec-reviewer, quality-reviewer, and final-reviewer prompts.
Added subagent status handling for DONE, DONE_WITH_CONCERNS, NEEDS_CONTEXT, and BLOCKED.
Added Final Implementation Review, Branch Resolution, and quality-gate handoff rules.
Updated brainstorm and plan with scope decomposition, section-by-section approval, and type/signature consistency checks.
Bumped VERSION and release surfaces from 2.10.0 to 2.11.0.
verification: python -m pytest packages/forge-core/tests -q -> PASS, 187 passed, 5 skipped
decisions_made: Harness-capable behavior changes require a verified failing test before implementation code.
Code written before RED must be deleted instead of adapted as reference.
Subagent execution remains packet-first and host-agnostic; hosts without subagents run the same lanes sequentially.
Independent sub-projects may enter plan only after their design section and shared contracts are approved.
risks: The repo-level one-shot pytest command has known module-name collisions across overlay suites, so verification is run per suite.
